title: Enabling Transport Subsidy (ETS)
text:
Enabling Transport Subsidy (ETS) - SupportGoWhere
Enabling Transport Subsidy (ETS)
Share link
Supports eligible Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents with disabilities who need to use dedicated transport provided by the Social Service Agencies (SSAs) to access the Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children (EIPIC), Special Education (SPED) schools, Day Activity Centres (DACs), Sheltered Workshops (SWs) and Special Student Care Centres (SSCCs).
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to 80% means-tested subsidy for transport fees
From July 2026, additional 15% subsidies for Singapore Citizens born in 1969 or earlier
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to 80% means-tested subsidy for transport fees

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen (SC) and Permanent Resident (PR)

Monthly household income per capita of $3,600 and below for SC

Monthly household income per capita of $2,600 and below for PR

Annual Value (AV) of residence reflected on NRIC of $21,000 and below for households with no income

Persons with disabilities on transport services provided by SSAs and attending Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children (EIPIC), Special Education (SPED) schools, Day Activity Centres (DACs), Sheltered Workshops (SWs). Special Student Care Centres (SSCCs), Enabling Skills for Life Programme (ESLP), Enabling Business Hubs (EBHs) and Enabling Service Hubs (ESHs).




From Jul 2026, SCs and PRs with monthly household income per capita of up to $4,800 will qualify for ETS.

How to apply?

Apply through SPED schools or SSAs that are arranging your transportation services. They will assist in conducting a means test for you and submit the subsidy request to SG Enable on your behalf.

Where can I find help?

View more details

SG Enable Hotline: 1800-8585-885

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

contactus@sgenable.sg

Enabling Village
20 Lengkok Bahru
#01-01, Singapore 159053

Scheme last updated 11 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
